name: Azure Well-Architected Framework — Security Pillar
description: Security design principles and recommendations from the Well-Architected Framework. Summaries are paraphrased.

controls.title: Establish a security baseline
controls.summary: Workloads should define and continuously measure against a security baseline aligned to compliance needs.
controls.remediation: Adopt MCSB as the baseline via Azure Policy initiative and track Secure Score improvements per sprint.

controls.title: Strict identity and access management
controls.summary: All access — human and workload — is authenticated, authorized, and time-scoped following least privilege.
controls.remediation: Entra ID everywhere, managed identities for workloads, PIM for privileged roles, and no standing broad access.

controls.title: Network isolation and segmentation
controls.summary: Traffic is controlled through segmentation, private connectivity, and inspection at trust boundaries.

check_hints.fail_message: Data services are not isolated behind private connectivity.

controls.remediation: Hub-spoke VNets, private endpoints for PaaS data services, Azure Firewall egress inspection, deny-by-default NSGs.

controls.title: Encrypt data everywhere
controls.summary: Data is encrypted in transit, at rest, and where feasible in use, with managed key lifecycle.
controls.remediation: TLS 1.2+, platform SSE with CMK where required, Key Vault-managed rotation, confidential computing for high-sensitivity workloads.

controls.title: Harden resources
controls.summary: Reduce attack surface by removing unused features, endpoints, and permissions.
controls.remediation: Disable FTP/remote debugging on App Service, remove public endpoints, minimal container base images, and close unused ports.

controls.title: Monitor with a threat-informed strategy
controls.summary: Telemetry, detection, and alerting are designed around realistic threats to the workload.
controls.remediation: Map detections to ATT&CK techniques relevant to the architecture; enable Defender plans and Sentinel analytics accordingly.

controls.title: Test and validate security
controls.summary: Security is validated continuously through testing: SAST, DAST, pentesting, and chaos/attack simulation.
controls.remediation: Security gates in CI, periodic pentests, attack path analysis in Defender CSPM, and purple-team exercises.

controls.title: Prepare incident response
controls.summary: The workload team can detect, respond to, and recover from incidents within agreed objectives.
controls.remediation: Workload-specific runbooks, contact matrix, automation for containment, and game-day rehearsals.
